signals.reasoning: This has become a failed management hold. The live BLUR long has only retained about 2% of peak P&L, is flagged urgent, and has effectively round-tripped the entire move. With veto still active, there is no reason to keep hoping for recovery.
BTC is liquid and stable, but the board remains no-trade and the active veto argues against opening fresh exposure. Neutral is the right stance.
ETH is mildly constructive but low-edge, with a neutral board and no compelling directional asymmetry. Not enough to matter here.
SOL has modest positive trend, but funding remains positive and the board is still no-trade. Nothing special enough to override the veto.
DASH has momentum and volume, but the board is still neutral and funding is positive. Watchlist only while the veto is active.
MON has become more momentum-positive and no longer offers a clean bearish asymmetry. It does not stand out enough on either side.
DYM is constructive but now lacks the strong negative-funding squeeze profile that made it attractive earlier. It drops to watch-only.
PYTH is one of the better secondary longs with improving board support, but it is still not strong enough to override the active veto.
WLFI is interesting because it is oversold with strongly negative funding, but the board is still mixed rather than clearly bullish. Worth watching, not worth pressing here.

ZEC is one of the strongest names on the sheet with real liquidity, strong momentum, and negative funding, but the active veto is exactly when extended continuation trades should be downgraded. Strong watchlist long, not a live trade.

TNSR still screens as a strong squeeze-continuation long mechanically, but the live geopolitical veto is a direct reason not to open fresh high-beta exposure. Strong watchlist long, not an active trade.
